## Create a forest plot {.unnumbered}


### Description

Draws a publication-ready forest plot from a data frame. Each row
represents one estimate — a study, a predictor, a model, a subgroup, or any
other unit of analysis. Rows whose <code>estimate</code> is <code>NA</code> are treated as
spacer/header rows: they produce no point or confidence interval, and their
label is rendered in bold if non-empty.



### Usage

<pre><code class='language-R'>forrest(
  data,
  estimate,
  lower,
  upper,
  label = NULL,
  group = NULL,
  is_summary = NULL,
  weight = NULL,
  ref_line = 0,
  log_scale = FALSE,
  xlim = NULL,
  xlab = "Estimate (95% CI)",
  title = NULL,
  header = NULL,
  cols = NULL,
  widths = NULL,
  stripe = FALSE,
  pch = 15,
  lwd = 2,
  cex = 1,
  col = NULL,
  legend_pos = "topright",
  ...
)
</code></pre>


### Arguments

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="data">data</code></td>
<td>
A data frame, tibble, or
data.table.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="estimate">estimate</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) for point estimates.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="lower">lower</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) for lower confidence interval bounds.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="upper">upper</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) for upper confidence interval bounds.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="label">label</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) for row labels displayed on the y-axis or
in the left text panel. If <code>NULL</code>, row numbers are used.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="group">group</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) for a grouping variable. Rows that share
a group value receive the same colour, and a legend is drawn automatically.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="is_summary">is_summary</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) of a logical vector. Rows where this
is <code>TRUE</code> are drawn as filled diamonds (e.g. pooled or overall estimates)
rather than squares with whiskers.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="weight">weight</code></td>
<td>
Column name (string) of numeric row weights. When provided,
point size scales as <code>cex * sqrt(weight / max(weight))</code>, so rows with
larger weights appear with a bigger marker. Weights for summary rows are
ignored (diamond size is fixed by <code>cex</code>).
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="ref_line">ref_line</code></td>
<td>
Numeric. Position of the vertical reference line (e.g. <code>0</code>
for differences, <code>1</code> for ratio measures on the natural scale). Set to
<code>NULL</code> to suppress. Default is <code>0</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="log_scale">log_scale</code></td>
<td>
Logical. If <code>TRUE</code>, apply a log transformation to the
x-axis. Useful when plotting odds ratios, hazard ratios, or risk ratios
on the natural scale. Default is <code>FALSE</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="xlim">xlim</code></td>
<td>
Numeric vector of length 2 giving x-axis limits. Computed from
the data when <code>NULL</code> (default). Confidence intervals that extend beyond
<code>xlim</code> are clipped at the axis boundary and an arrow is drawn to indicate
truncation.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="xlab">xlab</code></td>
<td>
Label for the x-axis. Default is <code>"Estimate (95% CI)"</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="title">title</code></td>
<td>
Plot title. Default is <code>NULL</code> (no title).
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="header">header</code></td>
<td>
Optional header string placed above the label column. When
<code>cols</code> is provided this appears above the left text panel; otherwise it is
drawn above the topmost row on the y-axis.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="cols">cols</code></td>
<td>
Named character vector specifying extra text columns to display
to the right of the plot. Names become column headers; values are column
names in <code>data</code>. Example:
<code>cols = c("OR (95\% CI)" = "or_text", "P" = "pval_text")</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="widths">widths</code></td>
<td>
Numeric vector of relative panel widths. When <code>cols</code> is
<code>NULL</code>, ignored. Otherwise, length must equal <code>2 + length(cols)</code>: label
panel, plot panel, then one entry per extra column. Sensible defaults are
chosen automatically.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="stripe">stripe</code></td>
<td>
Logical. If <code>TRUE</code>, alternate rows are shaded with a light
grey background to improve readability. Default is <code>FALSE</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="pch">pch</code></td>
<td>
Point character for non-summary rows. Default is <code>15</code> (filled
square), which is conventional for forest plots.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="lwd">lwd</code></td>
<td>
Line width for confidence interval whiskers. Default is <code>2</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="cex">cex</code></td>
<td>
Point size multiplier. Default is <code>1</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="col">col</code></td>
<td>
Colour or character vector of colours. When <code>NULL</code> (default) and
<code>group</code> is specified, the Okabe-Ito colorblind-safe palette is used. When
<code>NULL</code> and no <code>group</code>, a single dark colour is used.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="legend_pos">legend_pos</code></td>
<td>
Position of the legend when <code>group</code> is supplied. Passed
to <code>legend()</code>. Set to <code>NULL</code> to suppress the legend. Default is
<code>"topright"</code>.
</td></tr>
<tr><td style = "white-space: collapse; font-family: monospace; vertical-align: top"><code id="...">...</code></td>
<td>
Additional graphical parameters passed to the internal
<code>tinyplot::tinyplot()</code> call that sets up the main plot area (e.g.
<code>cex.axis</code>, <code>cex.lab</code>, <code>font.main</code>).
</td></tr>
</table>


### Value

Invisibly returns <code>NULL</code>. Called for its side effect of producing a
plot.



### Examples
```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, eval=TRUE}
library("forrest")

# Basic forest plot — results from a single regression model
dat <- data.frame(
  label    = c("Exposure A", "Exposure B", "Exposure C", "Exposure D"),
  estimate = c(0.42, -0.18, 0.31, -0.07),
  lower    = c(0.22, -0.38, 0.12, -0.24),
  upper    = c(0.62,  0.02, 0.50,  0.10)
)
forrest(dat,
  estimate = "estimate",
  lower    = "lower",
  upper    = "upper",
  label    = "label",
  xlab     = "Regression coefficient (95% CI)"
)

# Multiple models with subgroup headers and a text column
dat2 <- data.frame(
  label    = c(
    "Model 1", "  Exposure A", "  Exposure B",
    "", "Model 2", "  Exposure A", "  Exposure B"
  ),
  estimate = c(NA,  0.42, -0.18, NA, NA,  0.35, -0.14),
  lower    = c(NA,  0.22, -0.38, NA, NA,  0.16, -0.32),
  upper    = c(NA,  0.62,  0.02, NA, NA,  0.54,  0.04),
  is_sum   = rep(FALSE, 7),
  est_ci   = c(
    "", "0.42 (0.22, 0.62)", "-0.18 (-0.38, 0.02)",
    "", "", "0.35 (0.16, 0.54)", "-0.14 (-0.32, 0.04)"
  )
)
forrest(dat2,
  estimate   = "estimate",
  lower      = "lower",
  upper      = "upper",
  label      = "label",
  is_summary = "is_sum",
  header     = "Predictor",
  cols       = c("Coef (95% CI)" = "est_ci")
)



```
